basic_details.job_title: WII Recruitment 2026 - Apply Online for 14 Project Assistant, Project Associate and More Posts
basic_details.short_information: Wildlife Institute of India (WII) Various Research Positions Notification 2026 has been released under Advt. No. WII/ADVT. 2/RP-CELL/June, 2026 on the official website https://wii.gov.in/. A total of 14 vacancies have been announced through the WII Various Research Positions Recruitment 2026.

age_limit.age_details: As per rules (Relaxation for SC/ST, OBC)
age_limit.details: All Posts: Age relaxation as per rules. SC/ST candidates: up to 5 years. OBC candidates: up to 3 years.

qualification.education_qualification: Master's Degree, Ph.D., Bachelor's Degree in relevant fields
qualification.details: Project Associate I (Vegetation, GIS and Remote Sensing, Engineer/Visual Communication): Four Years Bachelor's Degree in Natural Sciences/Agricultural/Forestry Sciences OR Master's Degree in Natural Sciences/Agricultural Sciences OR Bachelor's degree in Veterinary Science OR Bachelor's Degree in Engineering/Technology OR Master's Degree in Engineering from a recognized institute/University. Project Research Scientist II (Non-Medical): First Class Master Degree in Science (Biology/Genomics/Genetics/Biochemistry/Microbiology/Biotechnology/Life Sciences/Veterinary Science or equivalent) with three years of experience or PhD OR Second Class Master Degree in Science with PhD and three years of experience. Project Technical Support – II: Three Years Bachelor Degree in Science (Life science/Wildlife science/Biology/Genomics/Genetics/Bioinformatics or equivalent) with three Years’ experience OR Master Degree in Science (Life science/Wildlife science/Biology/Genomics/Genetics/Bioinformatics or equivalent). Project Scientist-I: Ph.D. Degree in Wildlife Science/Zoology/Ecology/Life Sciences from a recognized institute/University. Senior Project Associate (Ecology): Master’s Degree in Wildlife Science/Zoology/Ecology/Life Sciences AND four years of experience (after obtaining Master’s degree) in R&D in Science & Technology Institutions or Scientific activities and Services. Project Associate I (Ecology): Master’s Degree in Wildlife Science/Zoology/Ecology/Life Sciences from a recognized institute/University. Project Associate I (RS/GIS): Master’s Degree in Natural Sciences (Wildlife Science/Zoology/Ecology/Environmental Sciences/Geoinformatics/Remote Sensing/Life Sciences)/Agricultural Sciences OR Bachelor’s Degree in Engineering/Technology (Computer Science/Information Technology/Remote Sensing/GIS/AI Technology/Data Science). Project Assistant: Bachelor’s Degree in Science (Wildlife Science/Zoology/Ecology/Environmental Sciences/Geoinformatics/Remote sensing/Life Sciences/Forestry) OR Bachelor's Degree in Science (Wildlife Science/Zoology/Ecology/Marine Sciences/Marine Biology/Environmental Sciences/Geoinformatics/Life Sciences/Agricultural/Forestry) from a recognized institute/University.

vacancy_details.Post Name: Project Associate I (GIS and Remote Sensing)

selection_process: Shortlisting based on qualifications and experience Online Interview Document Verification
how_to_apply: Candidates who meet the eligibility criteria can apply online for the WII Various Posts Recruitment 2026.
Applications must be submitted online on or before 30-06-2026.
To apply directly, use the Apply Online link provided in the Important Links section.
Alternatively, candidates may visit the official website of WII to complete the application.
Fill out the application form carefully with correct personal, educational, and contact details.
Upload required documents such as photograph, signature, and certificates in the prescribed format.
Pay the application fee online, if applicable.
Review all details carefully before submitting the form.
Take a printout or save a copy of the submitted application for future reference.

faq.question: Q1. What is the last date to apply for the WII Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The last date to apply for the WII Various Research Positions is 30 June 2026. Ensure your application is submitted online before this deadline.

faq.question: Q2. How many vacancies are available for the WII Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: There are a total of 14 vacancies for Various Research Positions announced by the Wildlife Institute of India for the year 2026.

faq.question: Q3. What is the application mode for WII Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The application mode for the WII Recruitment 2026 is strictly online. Candidates must submit their applications through the official WII website.

faq.question: Q4. What is the minimum qualification required for Project Assistant posts?
faq.answer: For Project Assistant posts, a Bachelor’s Degree in Science (Wildlife Science/Zoology/Ecology/Environmental Sciences/Geoinformatics/Remote sensing/Life Sciences/Forestry) from a recognized institute/University is required.

faq.question: Q5. What is the salary for Project Scientist-I?
faq.answer: The salary for the Project Scientist-I position is Rs. 77,000 plus HRA per month.

faq.question: Q6. Is there any age relaxation for reserved categories?
faq.answer: Yes, age relaxation is provided for SC/ST candidates up to 5 years and for OBC candidates up to 3 years, as per government rules.

faq.question: Q7. What is the selection process for these positions?
faq.answer: The selection process includes shortlisting based on qualifications and experience, followed by an online interview and document verification.

faq.question: Q8. Where can I find the official notification PDF?
faq.answer: The official notification PDF can be accessed through the "Important Links" section of this article. It contains detailed information about the recruitment.

faq.question: Q9. What is the application fee for General category candidates?
faq.answer: The application fee for General and OBC category candidates is Rs. 500/-. For SC/ST candidates, it is Rs. 100/-.

faq.question: Q10. What is the location of the Wildlife Institute of India?
faq.answer: The Wildlife Institute of India is located in Dehradun, Uttarakhand, with the pincode 248002.
